description_long: This object is a large, L-shaped solar panel array, composed of multiple rectangular photovoltaic modules arranged in a grid pattern. The surface is covered with dark, glassy solar cells, each separated by thin metallic grid lines, and the entire array is framed by a metallic border, likely aluminum. The structure appears to be designed for installation on a flat or slightly inclined surface, such as a rooftop or a dedicated solar platform. The array is relatively thin in profile, with a consistent, flat surface across all modules, and no visible mounting legs or supports in the provided views. The overall design is utilitarian, maximizing surface area for solar energy capture while maintaining a low profile. There are no visible electrical inverters or cabling on the exterior, suggesting these components may be integrated beneath the panel or housed separately.
description: A large, L-shaped solar panel array with a grid of dark photovoltaic modules framed in metal, designed for rooftop or platform installation. The surface is flat, glassy, and optimized for solar energy capture.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full L-shaped layout and the grid arrangement of the solar modules.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the thin profile and the metallic frame along the longer edge.
description_view_2: Front-on view emphasizing the length of the array and the uniformity of the solar cell grid.
description_view_3: Angled side view from the opposite end, showing the shorter segment of the L-shape and the consistent surface texture.
attribute_panel_orientation: flat, horizontal orientation with no visible tilt; orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or platform-mounted with an aluminum frame; no visible legs or posts
attribute_array_configuration: L-shaped array composed of multiple rows of rectangular panels arranged in a grid, approximately 8x3 and 3x3 sections
